Title: Identification of a novel link connecting indole-3-acetamide with abscisic acid biosynthesis and signaling

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Molybdenum cofactor sulfurase; NifS-like domain; pyridoxal phosphate-dependent sulfurase
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; Iberian natural accessions including Fue-2, Vis-0, Ace-0/Ace-3
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mediates abiotic stress responses and inhibits primary root growth at elevated levels; promotes seed dormancy
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by short-term IAM treatment in Col-0 seedlings; expressed in roots/seedlings
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: IAM-induced in Col-0 roots/seedlings; weak or absent induction in IAM-insensitive accessions carrying the alternative ABA3 allele
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; natural allele analysis; qRT-PCR; structural modeling; knockout mutant phenotyping (aba3-1); ABA signaling reporter assays; root growth assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous IAM treatment (10–20 µM); in vitro seedling growth; abiotic stress context discussed (cold, salt, osmotic stress)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IAM-triggered ABA biosynthesis/signaling and is required for full IAM-dependent primary root growth inhibition
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS identified ABA3 as a candidate gene associated with natural variation in IAM sensitivity. 2. IAM moderately induces ABA3 expression in Col-0 seedlings. 3. aba3-1 mutants show strongly reduced IAM-induced primary root inhibition (~12% vs ~30% in Col-0). 4. IAM-insensitive accessions carrying the ABA3 alternative allele show weak or absent ABA3 induction, linking ABA3 transcriptional responsiveness to the phenotype.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered ABA3 regulation may affect ABA-related traits such as seed dormancy and abiotic stress adaptation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ABA3 is a candidate target to modulate IAM–ABA crosstalk and root growth/stress-response traits, but manipulation may impact broader ABA-dependent traits including dormancy
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes ABA3 as a core causal factor, but its role is via activation of aldehyde oxidases such as AAO3 rather than direct ABA synthesis. Alternative ABA3 allele contains missense substitutions L102I, A150G, L267V and predicted reduced stability; authors conclude altered transcriptional regulation is the main cause of IAM insensitivity.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS using 1001 Genomes / GWAPP / easyGWAS; comparative transcriptomics using published IAM dataset and GEO GSE39384

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress signaling hormone controlling root growth inhibition and dormancy-associated responses
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutant phenotyping (abi5-7); root growth assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full IAM-triggered root growth inhibition, indicating IAM activates ABA signaling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. abi5-7 mutants showed only ~8% IAM-induced root growth reduction compared with ~30% in Col-0. 2. This demonstrates that ABA signaling downstream of IAM is necessary for the root phenotype. 3. ABI5 supports the conclusion that IAM activates ABA signaling.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating ABI5 may broadly alter ABA responses, stress sensitivity, and growth
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a signaling node for tuning ABA-dependent IAM responses, but likely pleiotropic
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although ABI5 is a regulator, the paper uses it mainly as an ABA signaling marker gene in reverse genetics rather than dissecting its direct metabolic targets.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Abiotic stress signaling and growth regulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA signaling output linked to root growth inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA; IAM indirectly through ABA biosynthesis/signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required signaling component for IAM-induced ABA response that inhibits root growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutant phenotyping (abi5-7); root growth assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ABI5 reduces IAM-triggered root growth inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The abi5-7 mutant is less sensitive to IAM-induced root growth inhibition. 2. This supports that IAM engages the ABA signaling pathway, not just ABA biosynthesis. 3. ABI5 is therefore a core signaling regulator connecting IAM response to the phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic effects on ABA-regulated growth and stress responses
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible node to modulate IAM/ABA growth responses, but broad ABA signaling effects require caution
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous IAM treatment under in vitro conditions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a reverse-genetic validation of ABA signaling involvement; direct target genes were not investigated in this study.
